textPlain: I don't have much to say about this post other than to vigorously agree!As an engineer who's full-stack and has frequently ended up doing product management, I think the main value I provide organizations is the ability to think holistically, from a product's core abstractions (the literal database schema), to how those are surfaced and interacted with by users, to how those are talked about by sales or marketing.Clear and consistent thinking across these dimensions is what makes some products "mysteriously" outperform others in the long run.
